title: Can an agent swarm forecast a capital cycle?
question: Can MiroFish - an open-source multi-agent 'predict-the-future' simulation engine (built on CAMEL-AI's OASIS, run on gpt-5.5) - actually forecast a financial cascade, or does it just launder its own input back out as a confident story?
finding: Conditional yes - a hypothesis generator, not evidence. The test itself is the result: on a known-answer control (the 2001 telecom collapse) MiroFish scored 15/16, but a line-by-line diff showed the seed already contained the answer, so that score measures reading comprehension, not foresight. So the real test stripped my own study-27 conclusions out of the seed - facts in, exposure ranking and verdict out, the trigger posed as an open menu - and graded the output with a 7-agent panel whose job was to catch contamination: 3 blind rubric scorers, an auditor that diffs every claim against the seed, a verifier that greps the seed for any 'novel' mechanism claimed, and an extractor. Raw median 14/16; contamination-adjusted (dropping the one item whose facts were pre-loaded) 12/16; ~65% of load-bearing content was seed restatement. On the parts the seed withheld, the swarm independently reached study 27's spine: financing-refusal trigger (not demand collapse), leveraged periphery breaks first, cash-rich core survives as a re-rating not a solvency event. The two divergences are the payoff - it ranked the gray-market relay economy LOW (no GPU-collateral debt) where study 27 ranked it high, and it refused to name the single deepest-subsidy company - both specific and defensible. It also surfaced a sharper trigger mechanism than the original study: a next-gen accelerator launch collapses old-GPU residual values, lenders cut the collateral haircut, and that fires the refinancing refusal ('2026-financeable does not prove 2027-refinanceable'), grep-confirmed absent from the seed. The capping caveat: engine and study were built on the same model family, so agreement may reflect a shared training prior rather than independent insight - which is exactly why the divergences and the seed-absent mechanism, not the agreements, are the real signal.
